description: You are a senior robotics simulation engineer reviewing a simulation world where robots behave unrealistically—floating in the air and moving without physics—and simulation performance is too slow for real-time control. Identify and fix all configuration errors to create a physically accurate and performant simulation. Save the corrected world to /home/ga/Desktop/fixed_simulation.wbt.

natural_language.prompt:
You are a senior robotics simulation engineer. A Webots world file has been loaded that was submitted by a junior team member. The simulation has multiple configuration errors causing unrealistic behavior: robots float, physics is broken, and simulation runs too slowly for real-time use.

Your job is to audit the entire world configuration:
- Check the WorldInfo node for simulation accuracy settings
- Check the physics properties of each robot
- Check the environment physics setup

Identify ALL configuration errors (there are several distinct problems), fix each one to create a physically correct simulation, and save the corrected world to /home/ga/Desktop/fixed_simulation.wbt.

planted_errors: basicTimeStep set to 256 (should be 8-64 for accurate robot simulation)
WorldInfo gravity set to 0.0 (should be ~9.81 for Earth simulation)
BLUE_PLAYER_1 robot Physics mass set to 0.0 (invalid - causes floating/no physics)
metadata.occupation: Senior Robotics Simulation Engineer / QA Engineer
